# NINA SEAPOP Seabird Colony Observations — Data Usability Assessment and Check-in

**Piotr Zaborowski**  
OGC Europe

*Generated with data-usability-checkin-agent (Sonnet 4.6) : 2026-05-05*  
*Building blocks generated by agent from live OBIS API sample*  
*Reviewed by human 2026-05-05*

---

## Abstract

This document records the data usability assessment and OGC Building Block check-in process for the NINA SEAPOP seabird colony observation dataset, published through the Ocean Biodiversity Information System (OBIS). The assessment follows the data-usability-checkin-agent workflow, which profiles the source data against SeaDOTs-style usability criteria, selects target building blocks from the `iliad-apis-features` repository, and produces three coordinated building blocks: a source-faithful schema (BB1), an OIM-aligned target model (BB2), and a discovery metadata record (BB3).

---

## 1. Source Dataset

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Dataset name | SEAPOP NINA |
| Programme | SEAPOP — Norwegian Seabird Population Monitoring |
| Institution | Norwegian Institute for Nature Research (NINA) |
| OBIS dataset ID | `561441ab-128b-4be7-8fce-de1ef79855da` |
| GBIF dataset key | `4c7a13a5-72b1-41ff-ad7c-8af40d3cc3cc` |
| DOI | [10.15468/gk9xqd](https://doi.org/10.15468/gk9xqd) |
| Records | 279,170 occurrence records |
| Coverage | Norway and Svalbard (3.4°–31.2°E, 5.9°–80.0°N) |
| Temporal extent | 2014–present (last updated 2025-09-22) |
| Taxa | Seabirds (class Aves) |
| License | CC-BY 4.0 |
| Sample endpoint | `https://api.obis.org/v3/occurrence?datasetid=561441ab-128b-4be7-8fce-de1ef79855da&size=20` |

---

## 2. Usability Assessment

| Criterion | Score | Rationale | Confidence | Evidence | Gap |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Relevance** | HIGH | Long-term seabird colony monitoring; coastal/marine context; relevant to ILIAD Digital Twin biodiversity use cases | High | 279K records across Norwegian coastline and Svalbard | None identified |
| **Representativeness** | MEDIUM | OBIS API response includes 13 synthetic enrichment fields not present in original source; core DwC fields are source-faithful | Medium | `bathymetry`, `shoredistance`, `sst`, `sss`, `aphiaID` confirmed as OBIS-pipeline additions | GBIF version (`4c7a13a5`) better preserves original; `eventDate` and `individualCount` stripped by OBIS |
| **Reliability** | HIGH | NINA/SEAPOP is the authoritative Norwegian seabird monitoring programme; CC-BY 4.0; DOI minted; active maintenance | High | DOI `10.15468/gk9xqd`; updated 2025-09-22 | Contact email not exposed via API |
| **Temporal validity** | HIGH | Dataset active 2014–present; suitable for long-term trend analysis | High | API last-modified 2025 | `eventDate` per record not available from OBIS API — use GBIF |
| **Ingestability** | MEDIUM | Source is flat JSON array (not GeoJSON); coordinate fields present; geometry wrapping needed; `ON_LAND` flags expected for colony records | Medium | `decimalLatitude`/`decimalLongitude` in every record; GeoJSON conversion straightforward | `eventDate`, `individualCount`, `samplingProtocol` absent from OBIS view |
| **Reusability in DT** | HIGH | Darwin Core fields map directly to `oim-bio-tdwg`; STAC/OGC Records catalog pattern covers discovery; colony identity can be formalised as `sosa:hasFeatureOfInterest` URI | High | Property overlap with `oim-bio-tdwg` context confirmed by vocab matching | Colony URI namespace (`seapop.no/colony/`) requires minting; no existing SEAPOP LOD endpoint |

### 2.1 Overall assessment

The SEAPOP dataset is well-suited for ingestion into the ILIAD DT framework. Its core Darwin Core fields align directly with `oim-bio-tdwg`, and the OBIS publication makes it globally discoverable. The primary ingestability blocker is that the OBIS API view strips `eventDate` and count fields; downstream workflows requiring temporal or abundance data must use the GBIF distribution.

The `ON_LAND` and `NO_DEPTH` quality flags produced by OBIS are expected for seabird colony records (birds nesting on cliffs and islands) and must not be treated as data quality failures.

---

## 3. Field Provenance Classification

All fields in the OBIS API response were classified into three categories:

### Darwin Core source fields (original NINA data)
`occurrenceID`, `basisOfRecord`, `catalogNumber`, `collectionCode`, `institutionCode`, `datasetID`, `occurrenceStatus`, `scientificName`, `scientificNameID`, `kingdom`, `phylum`, `class`, `order`, `family`, `genus`, `species`, `decimalLatitude`, `decimalLongitude`, `coordinateUncertaintyInMeters`, `country`, `stateProvince`, `locality`, `modified`, `originalScientificName`

### WoRMS taxonomy IDs (resolved by OBIS from `scientificNameID`)
`aphiaID`, `kingdomid`, `phylumid`, `classid`, `orderid`, `familyid`, `genusid`, `speciesid`, `subphylum`, `subphylumid`, `infraphylum`, `infraphylumid`, `megaclass`, `megaclassid`, `superclass`, `superclassid`

### OBIS synthetic enrichment (NOT from original data collector)
| Field | Source model | Note |
|---|---|---|
| `bathymetry` | EMODnet / GEBCO | Depth at observation point |
| `shoredistance` | GSHHS coastline | Negative values = inland |
| `sst` | WOA climatology | Sea surface temperature mean — not measured |
| `sss` | WOA climatology | Sea surface salinity mean — not measured |

### OBIS internal / pipeline fields
`id` (OBIS UUID), `dataset_id`, `node_id`, `dropped`, `absence`, `flags`, `category`, `unaccepted`, `marine`, `brackish`

---

## 4. Building Block Selection

### Target model (BB2) selection rationale

Candidate blocks ranked:

| Candidate | Overlap | Decision |
|---|---|---|
| `oim-bio-tdwg` | Direct Darwin Core field match across all taxonomy, location, and occurrence properties | **Selected** |
| `oim-obs` | SOSA observation pattern — applicable if count data were available | Not selected: count fields absent from OBIS view |
| `oim-coastal-feature` | Intertidal/coastal feature schema | Not selected: seabird colonies are occurrence records, not spatial features |

`oim-bio-tdwg` is the correct target. The only meaningful source-derived addition not already covered by Darwin Core is `colonyID` — a persistent URI for the nesting site, derived from the free-text `locality` field.

### Catalog (BB3) selection rationale

STAC Collection + OGC API Records pattern used, consistent with `stac_multidim_data` and `tst-inventory-seadots` patterns in the repository. STAC scientific extension (`sci:doi`, `sci:citation`) and prov extension (`prov:wasGeneratedBy`) applied.

---

## 5. Generated Building Blocks

Three building blocks were generated and written to `_sources/`:

### BB1 — Source Data Building Block

**Path:** `_sources/nina-seapop-source/`  
**ID:** `ogc.hosted.iliad.api.features.nina-seapop-source`  
**Purpose:** Source-faithful schema for the raw OBIS flat JSON occurrence record.

Captures the full OBIS API response structure including all 54 fields. Enrichment fields are included in the schema with descriptions that clearly document their non-source provenance. Intended as the source-side contract in a source→target transformation pipeline.

Key design decisions:
- Schema covers the OBIS JSON envelope (`total` + `results[]`) not just a single record
- Enrichment fields (`bathymetry`, `shoredistance`, `sst`, `sss`) are typed as `[number, "null"]` and carry description notes identifying their pipeline origin
- `ON_LAND` and `NO_DEPTH` flag values are documented as expected for this dataset type

### BB2 — OIM Target Model Building Block

**Path:** `_sources/nina-seapop-oim/`  
**ID:** `ogc.hosted.iliad.api.features.nina-seapop-oim`  
**Depends on:** `ogc.hosted.iliad.api.features.oim-bio-tdwg`  
**Purpose:** OIM-aligned GeoJSON Feature form for ILIAD/OGC API Features endpoints.

Extends `oim-bio-tdwg` with a single addition:

| Property | Type | Semantic mapping | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| `colonyID` | URI | `sosa:hasFeatureOfInterest` | Derived from source `locality` field |

All OBIS enrichment fields are excluded. The geometry is a GeoJSON Point from `decimalLatitude`/`decimalLongitude`.

The `colonyID` URI pattern `https://seapop.no/colony/<slug>` requires SEAPOP to mint and publish colony URIs. Until then, the URI can be minted locally following the slug pattern (lowercase `locality`, spaces → hyphens).

### BB3 — Catalog / Discovery Metadata Building Block

**Path:** `_sources/nina-seapop-catalog/`  
**ID:** `ogc.hosted.iliad.api.features.nina-seapop-catalog`  
**Purpose:** OGC API Records / STAC Collection metadata linking both distribution modes.

Encodes:
- Spatial bbox: Norway + Svalbard `[3.4, 57.0, 31.2, 80.0]`
- Temporal extent: `2014-01-01` – open
- Provider: NINA + OBIS + GBIF
- License: CC-BY 4.0
- DOI and full citation (STAC `sci` extension)
- Provenance: NINA as originating agent (STAC `prov` extension)
- Links to both OBIS source endpoint (BB1) and OIM schema (BB2)
- Link to GBIF distribution (recommended for `eventDate` and count fields)

---

## 6. Identified Metadata Gaps

| Gap | Severity | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| `eventDate` absent from OBIS API | **Blocking for temporal analysis** | Use GBIF distribution (`4c7a13a5`) |
| `individualCount` / `organismQuantity` absent from OBIS API | **Blocking for abundance analysis** | Use GBIF distribution |
| Colony URI namespace not live | Medium | SEAPOP to mint `https://seapop.no/colony/` URIs |
| OGC API Features endpoint not deployed | Medium | Update BB3 links when endpoint is available |
| Contact email not in API response | Low | Obtain from OBIS dataset landing page |
| Funding/project references not encoded | Low | SEAPOP partner list available at seapop.no |

---

## 6a. Transform: BB1 → BB2

**Type:** `jq`  
**Rationale:** The source is a flat JSON API response (`{total, results[]}`); jq handles structural reshaping with no dependencies and runs natively in the OGC bblocks postprocessor.

**Files:**
- `_sources/nina-seapop-source/transforms.yaml` — transform manifest
- `_sources/nina-seapop-source/transforms/obis-to-oim.jq` — jq script

**Mapping:**

| Source field | Target field | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `results[]` | `features[]` | Iterate OBIS results array |
| `decimalLongitude`, `decimalLatitude` | `geometry.coordinates` | `[lon, lat]` GeoJSON order |
| `occurrenceID` | `id` | Feature identifier |
| `locality` | `properties.colonyID` | Slugified to `https://seapop.no/colony/<slug>` |
| `basisOfRecord` | `properties.basisOfRecord` | |
| `catalogNumber` … `modified` | `properties.*` | All source DwC fields (see schema) |

**Excluded from output (OBIS enrichment / pipeline):**

`aphiaID`, `bathymetry`, `shoredistance`, `sst`, `sss`, `id`, `dataset_id`, `node_id`, `dropped`, `absence`, `flags`, `marine`, `brackish`, `category`, `unaccepted`, `*id` (WoRMS taxonomy IDs)

**Local test result:** PASS  
Assertions verified on 20-record live sample:
- FeatureCollection with 20 features
- Each feature: `type=Feature`, `geometry.type=Point`, `coordinates=[lon,lat]`
- `colonyID` = `https://seapop.no/colony/saltkjelsvikholman` (first record)
- No enrichment fields in `properties`
- All source fields present (`basisOfRecord`, `scientificName`, `decimalLatitude`, `decimalLongitude`, `locality`)
